title: Chicken Kurkure

instruction.title: Step 1: Marinate the Chicken
steps: In a bowl, mix chicken strips with ginger-garlic paste, chili powders, spices, lemon juice, and salt.
Add chopped coriander & mint. Mix well.
Beat in one egg. Cover and marinate at least 1 hour (overnight in fridge for best flavor).

instruction.title: Step 2: Prepare the Coating
steps: Crush cornflakes lightly (not powder, keep it flaky for crunch).
Mix with rice flour, cornflour, refined flour, salt, and pepper.

instruction.title: Step 3: Coat the Chicken
steps: Roll in the dry cornflakes mixture, press gently so flakes stick well.
Keep coated pieces on a tray. Rest 10 mins (helps coating set).

instruction.title: Step 4: Frying
steps: Fry chicken strips in batches, do not overcrowd.
Fry until golden brown & crispy (about 4–5 minutes per batch).
Drain on a wire rack or kitchen paper.

instruction.title: Step 5: Finishing Touch
steps: Sprinkle chaat masala immediately after frying.
Plate with mint mayo, lemon wedges, and microgreens (for a restaurant-style look).

chefTips: Double coating trick: Dip once more in beaten egg and re-coat in cornflakes for extra crunch.
Texture balance: Use both cornflakes & rice flour; cornflakes give crunch, rice flour keeps it light.
Oil control: Maintain oil temperature – too hot burns coating, too low makes it soggy.
Plating: Serve on a black slate/white plate, drizzle dip in zigzag, and add micro herbs for fine-dining effect.
